summary: Activity Feed Search & Note Updated Events feature testing complete. Backend: 8/8 tests passed (100%). Found and fixed a CRITICAL bug - 'Cannot access feedSearch before initialization' error in contact/[id].tsx caused by computed values being declared before state variables. Frontend now loads correctly with search functionality working.

critical.issue: FIXED: 'Cannot access feedSearch before initialization' ReferenceError - computed values were declared before state variables that they depend on

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Moved computed values (feedQuery, filteredEvents, visibleEvents) to after their state dependencies (feedSearch, events, showAllEvents) are declared at lines 209-219
Backend contact_events.py correctly handles note_updated event type with category='note', icon='document-text', color='#FF9F0A'
Frontend search filters on title, description, event_type, and getEventTitle() fallback - correctly client-side filtering
EVENT_CATEGORY_ICON at line 147 correctly maps 'note' category to document-text icon with #FF9F0A orange color
EVENT_TYPE_LABELS at line 121 includes 'note_updated' → 'Note Updated' for display
handleSave at lines 350-391 correctly logs note_updated event when notes change (compares with originalNotes)
Search input has data-testid='feed-search-input' at line 1128 for testing

success_rate.frontend: 100% after bug fix - all UI features working

seed_data_creation: Created note_updated events via backend tests for verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Activity Feed Search & Note Updated Events tested in iteration 54. CRITICAL BUG FIXED: ReferenceError in contact/[id].tsx - moved computed values after state declarations. Backend: POST /api/contacts/{user_id}/{contact_id}/events with event_type='note_updated' creates events correctly. GET returns all events including note_updated. Frontend: Search input filters by title/description/event_type. 'No matching events' message displays when no results. Note events show orange document-text icon.

bug_fix_details.root_cause: In contact/[id].tsx, computed values (feedQuery, filteredEvents, visibleEvents) at lines 181-190 were referencing feedSearch, events, and showAllEvents state variables before they were declared at lines 209-217
bug_fix_details.fix_applied: Moved the computed values to after the state declarations (now at lines 209-219 after events and feedSearch are declared)
bug_fix_details.verification: Contact detail page now loads without ReferenceError, search functionality works correctly
